accordion_sections.heading: Assessment
accordion_sections.html:
• Confirm likely groin hernia clinically and document side/site.
  • Assess reducibility and current pain severity.
  • Screen for emergency features: severe persistent pain, tender irreducible hernia, vomiting, and obstructive symptoms.
  • Record symptom burden, progression, and functional impact.
  • Consider differential diagnoses when presentation is atypical.

accordion_sections.heading: Primary care management
accordion_sections.html:
• Offer watchful waiting in selected low-risk reducible minimally symptomatic hernia (typically men) with shared decision-making.
  • Provide analgesia and activity advice.
  • Do not request routine ultrasound for clinically obvious uncomplicated inguinal hernia.
  • Use imaging selectively where diagnosis is uncertain or alternative pathology is suspected.
  • Provide clear safety-net advice for worsening pain, loss of reducibility, or obstructive features.

accordion_sections.heading: Who to refer
accordion_sections.html:
Hospital admission (same day)

  • Suspected strangulation or bowel obstruction.
  • Severe persistent pain with tender irreducible hernia, vomiting, abdominal distension, or systemic deterioration.

Urgent suspicion of cancer (USOC)

  • No routine USOC trigger in uncomplicated hernia pathway; use cancer pathway if separate suspicious features are present.

Urgent (non-cancer outpatient)

  • All suspected groin hernias in women.
  • Irreducible or partially reducible hernia without current emergency features.
  • Rapidly progressive symptoms or recurrent painful incarceration episodes.

Routine (outpatient)

  • Symptomatic reducible inguinal hernia (commonly men).
  • Diagnostic uncertainty requiring specialist review.

Include in referral

  • Side/site, reducibility, pain pattern, symptom duration and functional impact.
  • Any episodes suggesting incarceration/obstruction.
  • Comorbidity/frailty and patient treatment preference.

accordion_sections.heading: Who not to refer
accordion_sections.html: • Do not routinely refer minimally symptomatic reducible inguinal hernia (typically men) where watchful waiting has been agreed and safety-netting is in place.

accordion_sections.heading: References
accordion_sections.html:
1. Right Decisions/territorial board groin hernia pathways used in source run.
  2. NICE/CKS inguinal hernia primary care thresholds (sign-off recheck advised).
  3. RCS/ASGBI/BHS hernia guidance and commissioning-support documents.
